explicit_text: After the war started, my family and I were living in Oakland, California. We were forcibly taken from there to the Tanforan Assembly Center and then to Topaz in Utah. explicit_text: The Japanese American internment during World War II was a devastating experience for my family and me. We were uprooted from our home in Oakland and sent to camps like Tanforan and Topaz, losing our businesses and dignity. The impact on our parents was profound, with feelings of helplessness and pride wounded. Even after the war, reintegrating into society was difficult due to discrimination and taunting. I always hoped such injustice would never happen again, but sadly, history seems to echo in troubling ways.
